published_prior_art.cite: MogstadSantosTorgovitsky2018 and HanYang2024: finite convex/LP MTE bounds for policy-relevant weighted averages

repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest pid_dose_response_iv_v1 and pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1 have different kernels

published_prior_art.cite: MogstadSantosTorgovitsky2018/HanYang2024 give the finite LP substrate; the quotient-mixture clause is not a published theorem

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no CausalSmith catalogue entry states a multi-binary-IV MTE PRTE support frontier

published_prior_art.cite: Goff2024 identifies vector-monotone LATE-type parameters but does not analyze PRTE feasible-face width

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed distinction between observed-face zero width and convex policy-mixture support collapses: every chord row a_c is a row of A(P), hence a_c is in N_F(P), so conv{a_c}+N_F(P)=N_F(P).

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The second support-test failure is redundant as stated: because conv{a_c}+N_F(P)=N_F(P), the condition a_star notin conv{a_c}+N_F(P) is just rho_star(P) nonzero.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine the policy-mixture test before quotienting by the full feasible-face normal space; for example compare a_star to conv{a_c} modulo only active shape flat directions not already generated by the A(P) row equations.

Turn the Goff separation into a computable flagship frontier by naming the exact Goff parameter class and giving an observable threshold or finite-dimensional distance that sharply separates PRTE support from LATE point identification.
Add a worked nondegenerate m=2, K=2 or K=3 witness where the corrected residual is positive and all observed outcome/chord moments are compatible with nonconstant MTE variation.

recommended_next_step: Pivot the quotient definition; the current flagship mixture kernel is algebraically collapsed by the proposal's own definition of N_F(P).
